question.text: In the SEBI case against HSBC as DDP in the matter of Pathway Finance, what was the penalty imposed and under which section?

answer.text: Rs. 16 lakhs under Section 15HB of the SEBI Act
Rs. 1 lakh under Section 15HA of the SEBI Act
Rs. 10 lakhs under Section 15A(b) of the SEBI Act
Rs. 5 lakhs under Section 15A(b) of the SEBI Act

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In the SEBI order, a penalty of Rs. 5 lakhs was imposed upon HSBC-DDP under Section 15A(b) of the SEBI Act for violation of clause 5.1.4 of Operational guidelines of DDPs. The violation was that HSBC-DDP failed to promptly report to SEBI the change of domicile by Pathway Finance, with more than six months delay in intimation. **The Trap:** Rs. 16 lakhs was the penalty imposed on SHCIL (a different DDP) in a separate case. Candidates may confuse the two DDP penalty cases. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** DDPs carry a fiduciary responsibility to promptly report material changes about FPIs to SEBI, as they grant registration on behalf of SEBI.
